div.head: 269. Memorandum From the President’s Special Assistant (Rostow) to President JohnsonSource: Johnson Library, National Security File, Country File, Saudi Arabia, King Faisal Trip to U.S., 6/21/66-7/1/66. Confidential.

list.head: SUBJECT
list.item: Raising the Level of State Visits: The Upcoming Case of King Faisal

div.p: We have been exploring ways of stepping up the dignity of state visits and will have more to report later.

But Faisal’s visit in late June is not a good place to start, as the attached State Department memorandumAttached is an unsigned, undated memorandum on the subject “Downplaying Faisal’s Visit.” makes clear.

list.item: A big show for Faisal might intensify his struggle with Cairo and worsen our own poor relations with Nasser who has already read certain of our moves as an effort to back Faisal in a bloc against him.
Faisal has made good progress in developing his country since 1962; but it is still a mighty backward place. Moreover, he has not worked for a Yemen settlement with 100% good faith.

div.p: I recommend, therefore, that we play this visit correctly, and save any Billy Rose upgrading for a less ambiguous guest.
Play it correct and low keyNone of the options is checked.
